items.title: June 7, 1990: Pat O'Brien of CBS reports that the Boston Celtics had offered Duke head basketball coach Mike Krzyzewski $2.5 million for 5 years "to take over the team," then later reports (via the Charlotte Observer) that Coach K ultimately decided to stay at Duke University.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt: June 7, 1990: Pat O'Brien of CBS reports that the Boston Celtics had offered Duke head basketball coach Mike Krzyzewski $2.5 million for 5 years "to take over the team," then later reports (via the Charlotte Observer) that Coach K ultimately decided to stay at Duke University.

items.title: Stacey King always treated me with so much warmth and kindness when having me on his podcast over the years. He was just the best guy. Absolutely gutted to hear about his passing. Can’t imagine watching Bulls games without him.

items.title: Stephon Castle on Pop speaking to team before it left for NYC:
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Stephon Castle on Pop speaking to team before it left for NYC: 

"Pretty much his message was just let the last two games go.  It happened.  They were very winnable games.  We feel like we gave them those games.  We got to let those go and focus on the next one."

items.title: I don't have a sharply formulated take! KAT's production (< 20 PPG, 10+ RPG, ~5 APG) made me want to check it out. And then I thought, "inside-out playmaking big who defends better than people think, hit a new level playing off a high-volume scoring guard, and is disproving years
items.author: Dan Devine
items.body_excerpt: I don't have a sharply formulated take! KAT's production (< 20 PPG, 10+ RPG, ~5 APG) made me want to check it out. And then I thought, "inside-out playmaking big who defends better than people think, hit a new level playing off a high-volume scoring guard, and is disproving years of 'soft' insults."

media.description: The 76ers are promoting assistant general manager Jameer Nelson to be team's executive vice president of basketball operations under new president Mike Gansey, making the one-time NBA All-Star the No. 2 executive for the franchise, sources told ESPN.

items.title: June 7, 1988: Bill Laimbeer and Isiah Thomas hit back-to-back threes in the span of three seconds, and the Pistons take a 17-point halftime lead in Game 1 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 7, 1988: Bill Laimbeer and Isiah Thomas hit back-to-back threes in the span of three seconds, and the Pistons take a 17-point halftime lead in Game 1 of the NBA Finals.

Adrian Dantley scored 34 PTS and Thomas had 19 PTS/12 AST/4 STL in Detroit's 105-93 win over the Lakers.

items.title: RIP to an absolute legend, Stacey King.
items.author: Steph Noh
items.body_excerpt:
RIP to an absolute legend, Stacey King.

I hope Stacey is remembered as a man of the people. He would stay for 45 minutes to an hour after games every night for 20 years in order to take pictures and meet Bulls fans.
